description.value:
The Chapter 2 'cure', engineered from the researchers' note-sets — see @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-journals.JournalEntry.ch2SevenDaysJrn1.JournalEntryPage.iNq7BMF0o3J1WGli]{Curing Blood Veil} for crafting checks, time, and dose costs (~4 doses/day, or single doses at ~10 gp).

On taking a dose: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.fxBloodVeil00001]{Blood Veil} stops progressing, and its stage steps down by 1 each day until it ends — a suppression, not a cure. The mercy in its design: for 1 week after, the treated person spreads the cure by contact exactly as Blood Veil itself spread.

Suppresses, never cures: the cult keeps re-seeding the plague at a slow burn, and it only truly ends when Ileosa falls (Ch.6). Distributing doses recedes the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-journals.JournalEntry.cotctGuide000001.JournalEntryPage.R96jUmyAvMxCISST]{Epidemic Clock} by 1. (Removability hatch: at any chapter break the GM may rule this became a true cure.)

publication.title: Pathfinder: Curse of the Crimson Throne (PF2e conversion, private)
